meta.title: Conflict Studies and Insights Dashboard
meta.notes: Weekly reports are the source of truth. Monthly and quarterly views are computed by deterministic aggregation in app.js. Replace this file with a live API response of identical shape to go live (see README in conflict-dashboard.html).

phases.Active high-intensity war: Sustained large-scale combined-arms combat with strategic strike campaigns and significant casualties.
phases.Active low-intensity conflict: Persistent but limited kinetic activity: raids, strikes, and localised engagements below full-war intensity.
phases.Fragile ceasefire: A formal or tacit cessation of fire that holds imperfectly and is vulnerable to rapid breakdown.
phases.Contested de-escalation: Force levels declining amid disputes over terms, sequencing, and verification.
phases.Post-conflict ceasefire under strain: A negotiated halt holding after major combat, but stressed by violations and unresolved drivers.

domainTooltips.Fires & Strikes: Lethal effects: artillery, missiles, drones, air strikes and counter-fire.
domainTooltips.Intelligence: ISR, indications & warning, targeting cycle quality, and information operations.
domainTooltips.Manoeuvre: Ground/maritime movement, gains and losses of terrain, and freedom of movement.
domainTooltips.Protection: Air and missile defence, force protection, hardening, and civil defence.
domainTooltips.Sustainment: Logistics, munitions stocks, industrial output, and external resupply.
domainTooltips.Command & Control: Decision-making, coalition cohesion, political direction, and tempo.

divisions.name: General Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Analytical proxy for whole-of-force synthesis, joint planning, and strategic-level appreciation. Not a specific formation.
divisions.lens: Synthesise across all domains and theatres; identify second-order strategic implications for force planning.

divisions.name: 3rd Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Modelled as a combined-arms / mechanised-heavy formation. Doctrinal assumption for study only.
divisions.focus: Manoeuvre, protection, sustainment and C2 at scale
divisions.lens: Read developments for heavy combined-arms manoeuvre, armour survivability, and sustainment of mass.

divisions.name: 6th Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Modelled as a rapid-response / armour-and-aviation formation. Doctrinal assumption for study only.
divisions.focus: Speed, fires integration, intelligence and C2
divisions.lens: Read developments for tempo, deep-fires integration, and responsiveness of the targeting cycle.

divisions.name: 8th Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Modelled as a higher-readiness combined-arms formation. Doctrinal assumption for study only.
divisions.lens: Read developments for readiness posture, mobilisation timelines, and high-tempo employment.

divisions.name: 9th Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Modelled as a Guards / elite formation oriented to urban and high-tempo operations. Doctrinal assumption for study only.
divisions.focus: Readiness, urban operations, protection, high-tempo manoeuvre
divisions.lens: Read developments for urban combat lessons, force protection, and elite-formation readiness.

divisions.name: 21st Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Modelled as an infantry-heavy formation oriented to terrain control. Doctrinal assumption for study only.
divisions.focus: Force protection, terrain control, sustainment and tactical adaptation
divisions.lens: Read developments for dismounted force protection, terrain denial, and tactical adaptation.

divisions.name: 25th Division
divisions.doctrinalAssumption: Modelled as an infantry-heavy formation oriented to sustained ground holding. Doctrinal assumption for study only.
divisions.focus: Force protection, sustainment, terrain control and endurance
divisions.lens: Read developments for endurance of dismounted forces, logistics under fire, and area security.

Emerging.desc: Newly observed in theatre; limited, experimental employment.

Scaling.desc: Adoption rising quickly; production or transfer ramping.

Maturing.desc: Established, but effectiveness plateauing or being eroded by countermeasures.

Superseded.desc: Largely displaced by a more effective capability; residual use only.

Obsolete.desc: No longer effective or fielded in any meaningful way.

vectors.State transfer: Supplied by a state sponsor or ally.
vectors.Captured / reverse-engineered: Derived from captured systems or copied designs.

roles.Measure: An offensive/enabling capability that imposes a problem on the adversary.
roles.Countermeasure: A capability fielded primarily to defeat or degrade an adversary measure.
roles.Dual: Acts as both a measure and a countermeasure depending on context.

holding.desc: Countermeasure is currently coping with the threat in reporting.

stressed.desc: Countermeasure works but is no longer sufficient alone — under strain.

bypassed.desc: An adaptation defeats the countermeasure (e.g. fibre-optic FPV vs EW).

uncountered.desc: No effective countermeasure evidenced in current reporting.

safActions.Emulate: Proven and transferable — adopt the pattern.
safActions.Trial: Worth experimenting with under SAF conditions before adopting.
safActions.Review: Re-examine existing SOPs / assumptions in light of this.
safActions.Do not assume: Do not assume current SAF holdings defeat this.

sourceTypes.brief-derived: Signal computed directly from weekly-brief observations (traceable to editions).
sourceTypes.analyst-judged: Interpretation by the analyst — grounded in, but not stated verbatim by, the briefs.
sourceTypes.model-derived: Placeholder / seed estimate — not from the briefs.

heat.label: Heat
heat.inputs: Weekly-brief observation rows for the capability, each tagged with theatre and intensity (1–3).
heat.method: Recency-weighted sum of observation intensity (newest edition ×1.0 → oldest ×0.55), normalised 0–100 across all observed capabilities.
heat.fallback: A capability with zero brief observations is shown as un-observed — it is NOT assigned a modeled heat number.

lifecycle.label: Lifecycle
lifecycle.inputs: Analyst reading of where the capability sits (emerging → obsolete).
lifecycle.method: Analyst judgement — not computed from the briefs.

trend.label: Adoption trend
trend.inputs: Count of brief observations in the recent half vs the earlier half of the loaded editions.
trend.method: Rising if recent > 1.25× earlier, Declining if < 0.75×, otherwise Steady.

timeToCounter.label: Time-to-counter
timeToCounter.inputs: Would require a dated first-use and a dated first-effective-counter from the briefs.
timeToCounter.method: Not reliably derivable from the current briefs — removed as a live metric.
timeToCounter.fallback: Shown only where an analyst explicitly records it, badged analyst-judged.

supersession.label: Supersession
supersession.inputs: Analyst reading of which capability displaced which, and how completely.
supersession.method: Analyst judgement, graded Fully superseded / Partially displaced / Still valid in niche.

proliferation.label: Cross-theatre proliferation
proliferation.inputs: Distinct theatres in which the capability is observed in the briefs (scoped to its theatres of relevance).
proliferation.method: Theatre count is counted from brief observations; the spread / transferability commentary is analyst-judged.
proliferation.fallback: Theatre count is brief-derived; the 'why it spreads / limits' commentary is analyst-judged.

capabilityContests.title: RF-controlled FPV drone vs tactical EW
capabilityContests.threatens: Armour, trench lines and exposed movement in the close fight (last 100–1,000 m).
capabilityContests.observedEffect: Trench jammers and man-portable EW degrade RF-link FPVs, but reporting shows they no longer defeat them reliably — operators shift frequencies, fly pre-programmed terminal runs and saturate.
capabilityContests.adaptationBypass: Fibre-optic FPVs remove the RF link entirely, defeating jamming outright (see the fibre-optic contest).
capabilityContests.judgmentNote: EW alone is no longer sufficient against the RF FPV threat.
capabilityContests.safImplication: Layer kinetic defeat (C-UAS guns, shotguns, nets, overhead cover) with EW; do not treat jamming as a complete answer.

capabilityContests.title: Fibre-optic FPV drone vs electronic warfare
capabilityContests.threatens: The same close-fight targets as RF FPVs, plus rear-area and rotary-wing targets — now immune to jamming.
capabilityContests.countermeasureNote: Currently uncountered in current reporting — defeat depends on physical / kinetic means and spotting the fibre, none evidenced at scale.
capabilityContests.observedEffect: Reporting shows fibre-optic FPVs operating through EW that would defeat RF drones — including against Iron Dome sites and in rear areas.
capabilityContests.adaptationBypass: The fibre spool IS the adaptation to EW. Emerging counters (nets, kinetic C-UAS, cutting the line) are not yet evidenced at scale.
capabilityContests.judgmentNote: Currently uncountered in current reporting — EW does not defeat it.
capabilityContests.safImplication: Assume EW-immune FPVs in the close fight; invest in kinetic C-UAS, overhead protection and counter-recce rather than relying on jamming.

capabilityContests.title: Shahed-136 / Geran-2 mass strike vs layered air defence
capabilityContests.threatens: Energy and logistics infrastructure, rear-area C2 and population centres via massed one-way attack drones.
capabilityContests.observedEffect: Layered AD (Patriot / IRIS-T / NASAMS, guns, EW) intercepts a large share, but 200+ drone packages still leak through and impose an unfavourable cost-exchange on defenders.
capabilityContests.adaptationBypass: Attackers scale raid size, mix in decoys and ballistic / cruise rounds, and time strikes to exhaust interceptor magazines.
capabilityContests.judgmentNote: The counter works per-shot but is stressed by cost-exchange and magazine depth.
capabilityContests.safImplication: Prioritise low-cost layered defeat (guns / EW / interceptor-UAS) and magazine depth over exquisite interceptors alone.

capabilityContests.title: Iranian ballistic / cruise + Shahed saturation vs Iron Dome / Arrow
capabilityContests.threatens: Israeli and GCC military and civilian targets via mixed ballistic, cruise and drone salvos designed to saturate defences.
capabilityContests.observedEffect: Reporting shows high interception rates but visible saturation pressure — direct Iran–Israel exchanges and Houthi re-entry stretch the defensive architecture.
capabilityContests.adaptationBypass: Iran alternates between salvo mass and coercive 'rule-setting' (e.g. Hormuz), mixing profiles to complicate the intercept problem.
capabilityContests.judgmentNote: Layered defence is holding but visibly stressed by saturation.
capabilityContests.safImplication: Plan for saturation: defence-in-depth, interceptor economy and resilience of defended assets — not single-layer reliance.

capabilityContests.title: Naval uncrewed surface vessels vs surface combatants / patrols
capabilityContests.threatens: Surface combatants, ports and chokepoint freedom of movement, using cheap explosive USVs.
capabilityContests.observedEffect: Analyst assessment: USVs have repeatedly denied a stronger navy freedom of action; dedicated counters (patrol helos, close-in guns) are only partial.
capabilityContests.adaptationBypass: Swarming, night attacks and aerial-USV teaming complicate point defence.
capabilityContests.judgmentNote: Analyst-judged — not cleanly isolated in the currently loaded briefs.
capabilityContests.safImplication: Coastal / chokepoint forces need layered close-in defeat and distributed basing against cheap USV swarms.

capabilityContests.title: Subterranean tunnel networks vs detection & breach
capabilityContests.threatens: Negates surface ISR / air overmatch; enables protected movement, storage and ambush in dense / urban terrain.
capabilityContests.observedEffect: Analyst assessment: detection-and-breach is manpower-intensive and slow; surface overmatch does not translate underground.
capabilityContests.adaptationBypass: Deeper, compartmented and re-dug networks defeat one-off clearance.
capabilityContests.judgmentNote: Analyst-judged — limited direct tunnel reporting in the current editions.
capabilityContests.safImplication: Invest in subterranean detection, breaching and the manpower / serials to clear and hold.

capabilities.name: Shahed-136 / Geran-2
capabilities.saf: Mass cheap one-way attack drones force a high-low cost-exchange problem for air defence; drives need for layered, low-cost interceptors and EW.
capabilities.note: Iranian design now produced at scale in Russia; the archetypal proliferating long-range strike capability.
capabilities.spreadWhy: Cheap, mass-producible one-way attack drone with a low skill floor — attractive to any actor wanting long-range strike on the cheap.
capabilities.transferLimits: Needs a production base or state transfer; defeated in part by layered low-cost AD and EW.
capabilities.safRelevance: High — SAF must plan affordable layered AD against massed cheap drones.

capabilities.name: RF-controlled FPV drone
capabilities.saf: Cheap precision under 10km collapses the close fight; mandates EW at platoon level, overhead cover and counter-UAS drills.
capabilities.note: Dominant tactical killer but vulnerable to RF jamming, driving the move to fibre-optic control.
capabilities.niche: RF FPVs remain dominant where fibre is unavailable, ranges exceed the spool, or cost/availability matter more than EW immunity.

capabilities.name: Fibre-optic FPV drone
capabilities.saf: Immune to RF jamming — a countermeasure to EW that is itself a strike measure; signals limits of EW-only counter-UAS and need for kinetic/last-ditch defeat.
capabilities.note: Clearest current example of a countermeasure-to-the-countermeasure (defeats trench EW).

capabilities.name: UMPK glide bomb
capabilities.saf: Stand-off mass fires from inside friendly airspace; hard to counter without pushing GBAD/airpower forward — a persistent un-countered problem.
capabilities.note: Cheap kits turn legacy bombs into stand-off munitions; among the hardest measures to counter.

capabilities.name: HIMARS / GMLRS
capabilities.saf: Demonstrates how GNSS jamming erodes precision-fires effectiveness over time; argues for alt-navigation and resilient PNT.
capabilities.note: Initial decisive effect degraded as Russian GNSS jamming/spoofing matured.

capabilities.name: ATACMS / Storm Shadow
capabilities.saf: Deep strike forces dispersal of rear logistics/airpower; counter is to move assets out of range — a manoeuvre/sustainment lesson.
capabilities.note: Drives Russian relocation of high-value assets beyond range.

capabilities.name: Naval USV (Magura / Sea Baby)
capabilities.saf: A navy-less actor achieved sea denial against a conventional fleet; major lesson for small-craft swarm threats to naval and coastal assets.
capabilities.note: Pushed the Black Sea Fleet out of the western basin; concept now diffusing (e.g. Red Sea).

capabilities.name: Recon UAV (Orlan / ZALA)
capabilities.saf: Persistent ISR closes the sensor-to-shooter loop; counter-ISR and emission control become survival skills.
capabilities.note: Backbone of the reconnaissance-strike complex.

capabilities.name: COTS quadcopter ISR (DJI-class)
capabilities.saf: Early COTS adaptation, now displaced by purpose-built ISR/FPV — a clean example of supersession to track.
capabilities.note: Vulnerable to tracking/geofencing and jamming; overtaken by military FPV and recon UAVs.
capabilities.niche: Residual use only for cheap, very-short-range observation where milspec ISR is absent.

capabilities.name: Patriot / IRIS-T / NASAMS
capabilities.saf: High-end intercept works but is interceptor-stock and cost constrained; magazine depth and cost-per-kill are the binding planning factors.
capabilities.note: Effective against ballistic and cruise threats; saturation and interceptor economics are the limiting risk.
capabilities.spreadWhy: Premier transferred SAM; spreads via alliance supply.
capabilities.transferLimits: Scarce, expensive and magazine-limited under saturation.

capabilities.name: Tactical EW / trench jammers
capabilities.saf: The dominant counter-UAS method — but fibre-optic FPV defeats it, so EW must be paired with kinetic last-ditch defeat.
capabilities.note: Now itself being countered by EW-immune fibre-optic drones.

capabilities.name: GNSS jamming / spoofing
capabilities.saf: Degrades GNSS-guided munitions and navigation; drives demand for resilient PNT and inertial/terminal seekers.
capabilities.note: Quietly one of the most consequential countermeasures of the war.

capabilities.name: Interceptor UAV
capabilities.saf: A low-cost kinetic answer to the Shahed cost-exchange problem; watch as a fast-rising counter-UAS option.
capabilities.note: Promising low-cost intercept; scaling and reliability still maturing.

capabilities.name: Mobile AAA / SPAAG (Gepard)
capabilities.saf: Gun-based defeat is cost-favourable against drones; revalidates SHORAD/gun AD that many militaries had divested.
capabilities.note: Cost-effective Shahed defeat; ammunition supply is the constraint.

capabilities.name: Forward long-range SAM
capabilities.saf: Only credible counter to glide bombs is to threaten the launch aircraft — high-risk forward GBAD employment.
capabilities.note: Attempts to hold launch platforms at risk; exposes scarce SAMs to attrition.

capabilities.name: Strategic SAM (S-400)
capabilities.saf: High-end SAM that is itself a priority target for deep strike — the SEAD/IADS duel in microcosm.
capabilities.note: Both a countermeasure and a high-value target.

capabilities.name: Iranian ballistic / cruise + Shahed mix
capabilities.saf: Saturation salvos mixing ballistic, cruise and drones stress layered defence depth; magazine depth is decisive.
capabilities.note: Proliferation hub: the Shahed family and missile tech radiate from here to multiple theatres and proxies.
capabilities.spreadWhy: Mixed ballistic / cruise / drone salvo doctrine spreading via Iranian transfer and emulation by proxies.
capabilities.transferLimits: Requires missile inventory and targeting; partly defeated by layered ABM.
capabilities.safRelevance: Medium — informs saturation and interceptor-economy planning.

capabilities.name: Arrow / THAAD layered defence
capabilities.saf: Demonstrates multi-tier BMD intercepting large salvos; cost-per-engagement and interceptor stockpiles are the watch items.
capabilities.note: High intercept rates against complex salvos; sustainment economics are the limiting factor.

capabilities.name: Iron Dome
capabilities.saf: Mature C-RAM with strong intercept rates, but large simultaneous salvos remain the saturation risk.
capabilities.note: Effectiveness eroded only by mass simultaneous launches.
capabilities.spreadWhy: Proven point / area defence against rockets and short-range threats; exportable.
capabilities.transferLimits: Expensive per intercept; stressed by saturation and FPV / top-attack.
capabilities.safRelevance: Medium — informs SAF interceptor-economy and layering.

capabilities.name: Unguided/short-range rockets
capabilities.saf: Saturation is the only path through modern C-RAM; drives interest in PGMs over volume — a measure being superseded by precision.
capabilities.note: Being displaced by precision-guided systems where available.
capabilities.niche: Cheap unguided rockets remain useful for short-range saturation and harassment despite precision alternatives.

capabilities.name: Trophy active protection system
capabilities.saf: Hard-kill APS defeats RPG/ATGM well but top-attack drones probe its limits — APS plus overhead cover is the emerging standard.
capabilities.note: Strong vs direct ATGM/RPG; top-attack FPV is the open challenge.

capabilities.name: Anti-tank guided missile
capabilities.saf: Classic anti-armour increasingly defeated by APS and out-ranged by cheap FPV — a measure under pressure from both sides.
capabilities.note: Being superseded for many tasks by cheaper FPV drones.
capabilities.niche: ATGMs retain the edge at longer range, in clear LOS, against targets beyond FPV endurance, and where EW would defeat FPVs.
capabilities.spreadWhy: Mature, widely transferred, low training burden.
capabilities.transferLimits: Range / LOS dependent; APS and FPVs erode its niche.
capabilities.safRelevance: Medium — APS fit and anti-armour tactics review.

capabilities.name: Subterranean tunnel networks
capabilities.saf: Subterranean denial negates air/ISR overmatch; demands specialised breach, mapping and subterranean-warfare capabilities.
capabilities.note: Persistent measure that is slow and costly to counter.
capabilities.threatens: Surface ISR / air overmatch; enables protected movement

capabilities.name: Tunnel detection & breach
capabilities.saf: Robotics, sensing and engineered defeat for tunnels; a slow-burning but doctrinally important counter to invest in.
capabilities.note: Mix of robotics, sensing, flooding and demolition.

capabilities.name: Conventional surface combatant
capabilities.saf: High-value crewed surface units are increasingly held at risk by cheap USVs/missiles in confined seas — a sea-control rethink.
capabilities.note: Forced into port or out of contested waters by cheap unmanned threats.
capabilities.threatens: Sea control & naval presence (now contested by USVs)
capabilities.spreadWhy: Conventional naval presence — less 'proliferating' than increasingly 'contested' by cheap USVs.
capabilities.transferLimits: High cost; vulnerable to cheap uncrewed attack.

capabilities.name: Maritime patrol helicopter
capabilities.saf: Rotary patrol is a partial counter to USVs; small fast surface threats still lack a cheap, scalable defeat.
capabilities.note: Only a partial answer to the USV problem.

capabilities.name: Border surveillance ISR
capabilities.saf: Low-cost ISR for border transparency; a confidence-building enabler more than a strike capability.
capabilities.note: Rising adoption for border monitoring; not yet a kinetic dynamic.

capabilities.name: Tube & SP artillery
capabilities.saf: Still the mass-fires backbone, but counter-battery driven by drone ISR makes shoot-and-scoot and dispersion essential.
capabilities.note: Enduring, but survivability now hinges on countering ISR.
capabilities.threatens: Massed targets, fixed positions & counter-battery duels
capabilities.spreadWhy: Universal indirect fire — present in every theatre.
capabilities.transferLimits: Counter-battery and drone spotting expose static guns quickly.

formationGroups.name: Manoeuvre
formationGroups.focus: Local gains, terrain seizure/retention, mechanised employment, urban and defended-terrain adaptation, force preservation and tempo.

formationGroups.name: Sense Strike
formationGroups.focus: Sensor-to-shooter loops, targeting effectiveness, counter-ISR, strike massing/saturation, deep strike and targeting support.

formationGroups.name: Combat Support
formationGroups.focus: Engineering survivability/mobility, communications resilience, command-post dispersion, signature management, protection of nodes.

formationGroups.name: Expeditionary
formationGroups.focus: Rapid/politically-constrained action, raids and interdiction, small-unit initiative, protection in non-permissive settings.

monthlyBlufByMonth.M1: April's lessons centre on stand-off mass fires (glide bombs) punishing static defence, persistent drone ISR driving counter-battery and forcing shoot-and-scoot, strikes reaching logistics depth, and the revalidation of gun-based SHORAD against cheap drones. Adaptation across every group converged on dispersion, hardening and signature discipline.
monthlyBlufByMonth.M2: May's lessons are dominated by the drone–counter-drone duel reaching the close fight — fibre-optic FPV defeating trench electronic warfare — alongside the rising cost of static positions under top-attack drones and stand-off fires, and the decisiveness of a fast sensor-to-shooter loop. Force preservation, command-post survivability, layered counter-UAS and small-craft / limited-objective action under political constraint recur across all four formation groups.

monthlyInsights.title: Windowed massing inside ISR blind spots
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Massed combined-arms assaults only achieved local gains when timed to gaps in adversary ISR and preceded by suppression — standing concentrations were detected and struck.
experiment: Assemble–strike–disperse timings rehearsed against a live counter-UAS/ISR 'clock'.
Brigade decision-support templates that key H-hour to observed ISR gaps.
train: Forming-up-point occupation under persistent overhead surveillance.
Rapid post-assault dispersal before counter-fire windows close.
adjustSOP: Cap assembly-area dwell time; mandate dispersal triggers in the assault SOP.

findings.text: A 15+ vehicle mechanised counterattack penetrated ~5km by exploiting an adversary in regrouping — massed assault still decisive in a window.
Where mass was not windowed, offensives showed no confirmed advances as tempo peaked and logistics degraded.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Massed combined-arms assaults only achieved local gains when timed to gaps in adversary ISR and preceded by suppression — standing concentrations were detected and struck.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Assembly areas and forming-up points are detected and struck before H-hour, so massing in the open invites destruction unless tightly windowed and dispersed.

monthlyInsights.title: Armour survivability vs top-attack drones
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Active protection defeated direct-fire ATGM/RPG but top-attack FPV exploited the overhead gap — crews need a layered answer, not APS alone.
experiment: Cope-cage + APS + section-level counter-UAS combinations on vehicles.
Dedicated vehicle 'drone sentry' role within each crew.
train: Overhead-threat reaction drills (halt, disperse, engage/screen).
Counter-UAS shoot at section level integrated into crew gunnery.
adjustSOP: Task-organise counter-UAS to section; add overhead-threat actions to mounted SOPs.

sources.label: BBC Verify — AI-enabled Hornet strike drones

findings.text: Rear and forward concentrations are exposed; the adversary's drone-reconnaissance advantage has eroded since 2024, so vehicles are persistently observed.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Active protection defeated direct-fire ATGM/RPG but top-attack FPV exploited the overhead gap — crews need a layered answer, not APS alone.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Crews face an overhead threat their armour and active protection were not built to defeat, driving vehicle losses in the advance.

monthlyInsights.title: Fibre-optic FPV reopens the close fight
monthlyInsights.soWhat: EW-immune fibre-optic FPV bypassed trench jamming — sub-units cannot rely on EW alone for last-ditch counter-UAS.
experiment: Kinetic last-ditch defeat (shotguns, interceptor drones, gun screens) alongside EW.
Passive overhead cover on routes and in defensive positions.
train: Counter-UAS battle drill that assumes EW has been defeated.
Visual/acoustic early warning at section level.
adjustSOP: Add a kinetic counter-UAS layer to the defensive-position SOP; do not list EW as sole defeat.

findings.text: The reconnaissance-strike advantage now shifts week to week — the close-range drone duel is continuous and mutual, not won once.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: EW-immune fibre-optic FPV bypassed trench jamming — sub-units cannot rely on EW alone for last-ditch counter-UAS.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: EW can no longer be relied on to keep the close fight safe — sub-units must defeat lethal drones kinetically in the last 100m.

monthlyInsights.title: Depth and reserves blunt offensive culmination
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Layered defences, obstacle belts and timely reserve commitment forced a two-axis push to culminate without breakthrough — depth, not the forward line, decided it.
experiment: Named-area-of-interest triggers tied to reserve-commitment decisions.
Obstacle-belt + counter-mobility integration with the fires plan.
train: Reserve-demand and commitment decision drills in the defensive battle.
Hand-over between defending and counter-attack forces.
adjustSOP: Codify reserve-commitment triggers and obstacle hand-over in the defensive SOP.

findings.text: Artillery and armour were reluctant to deploy under drone interdiction, enabling defender counterattacks — depth plus counter-fire blunts the attacker.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Layered defences, obstacle belts and timely reserve commitment forced a two-axis push to culminate without breakthrough — depth, not the forward line, decided it.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: An attacker seeks a single decisive penetration; without depth and timely reserves the defence breaks at the forward edge.

monthlyInsights.title: Static positions punished by stand-off glide bombs
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Guided glide bombs made fixed positions costly — survivability now depends on dispersion, hardening and deception, not occupation time.
experiment: Decoy positions and thermal/EM decoys at company strongpoints.
Position-rotation rhythm based on detection risk.
train: Survivability-move drills under stand-off precision threat.
Hasty hardening and overhead protection.
adjustSOP: Set maximum position-occupation times; require alternate/decoy positions in defence SOPs.

findings.text: Mid-range drone strikes (35–90km) routinely hit depots and routes lacking air-defence cover — static, uncovered positions are attritable.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Guided glide bombs made fixed positions costly — survivability now depends on dispersion, hardening and deception, not occupation time.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Any static, un-hardened position is attritable from stand-off range, so holding ground without dispersal and deception is a liability.

monthlyInsights.title: Subterranean and urban terrain negates overmatch
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Defended urban and tunnel terrain denied air/ISR overmatch and forced slow, costly combined-arms clearance.
experiment: Combined-arms urban breaching teams with engineer and counter-subterranean enablers.
Small-UAS for internal-building and tunnel reconnaissance.
train: Subterranean clearance and urban breaching as a graded ICT serial.
Fighting in degraded comms/GPS inside structures.
adjustSOP: Add subterranean threat handling and urban breach task-org to the clearance SOP.

findings.text: Extensive tunnel-network destruction was a required, deliberate task to undermine defensive mobility and command — not an improvised one.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Defended urban and tunnel terrain denied air/ISR overmatch and forced slow, costly combined-arms clearance.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Air and ISR overmatch evaporates in tunnels and dense urban terrain, forcing slow, manpower-intensive clearance under constant ambush risk.

monthlyInsights.title: Compress the sensor-to-shooter loop on fleeting targets
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Deep ISR fused to long-range fires struck fuel/rail nodes; the decisive variable was the time from detection to effect on perishable targets.
experiment: Pre-delegated engagement authority for nominated target sets.
Single fused sensor-shooter chat/feed at the targeting cell.
train: Dynamic-targeting drills against time-sensitive targets with a stopwatch.
Sensor-to-shooter rehearsals across ISR and fires elements.
adjustSOP: Define engagement-authority delegation and kill-chain timings in the targeting SOP.

sources.label: Al Jazeera — Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries

findings.text: Coordinated deep strikes hit refineries and the Lazarevo pumping station across five regions — fused targeting struck nodes at theatre depth.
The campaign disrupted logistics theatre-wide and forced Crimea fuel rationing — speed against perishable nodes paid off.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Deep ISR fused to long-range fires struck fuel/rail nodes; the decisive variable was the time from detection to effect on perishable targets.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: High-value targets are fleeting; a slow kill chain means the target has moved before fires arrive, wasting munitions and intelligence.

monthlyInsights.title: Precision fires degrade under GNSS jamming
monthlyInsights.soWhat: GNSS-guided rocket accuracy fell as jamming/spoofing matured — PNT dependence is a single point of failure for the fires system.
experiment: Alternate-PNT and terminal-seeker munitions in the fires mix.
Degraded-PNT observed-fire procedures.
train: Massing accurate fires under simulated GNSS denial.
Rapid switch to map/observed correction when PNT is lost.
adjustSOP: Add a GNSS-denied annex to the fire-support SOP with alternate-PNT procedures.
monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: GNSS-guided rocket accuracy fell as jamming/spoofing matured — PNT dependence is a single point of failure for the fires system.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Fires that depend on GNSS lose accuracy exactly when needed most, degrading the formation's primary killing system under jamming.

monthlyInsights.title: Saturation salvos overwhelm layered air defence
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Mixed ballistic/cruise/drone salvos stressed interceptor magazines — depth, cueing and engagement sequencing mattered more than single-shot capability.
experiment: Engagement-sequencing and weapon-target pairing against mixed salvos.
Low-cost defeat options to protect high-end magazines.
train: Saturation-attack defence drills with magazine-management decisions.
Sensor cueing and hand-off between AD layers.
adjustSOP: Codify weapon-target pairing and magazine-management rules in the AD SOP.
monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Mixed ballistic/cruise/drone salvos stressed interceptor magazines — depth, cueing and engagement sequencing mattered more than single-shot capability.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Mixed ballistic/cruise/drone salvos exhaust interceptor magazines, so thin or poorly-sequenced air defence is overwhelmed and key assets are lost.

monthlyInsights.title: Counter-ISR is as important as ISR
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Killing reconnaissance UAVs and enforcing emission control broke the adversary kill chain before fires were called.
experiment: Routine counter-recon screens and EMCON states for sub-units.
Decoys and dummy emitters to absorb adversary ISR.
train: Find-and-defeat reconnaissance-UAV drills.
Emission-control discipline as a routine state.
adjustSOP: Make counter-recon and EMCON a standing paragraph in every scheme of manoeuvre.

findings.text: The adversary's drone-recon advantage has eroded — counter-reconnaissance and emission control now materially break the kill chain.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Killing reconnaissance UAVs and enforcing emission control broke the adversary kill chain before fires were called.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: If the adversary's reconnaissance survives, its fires find you first — failing to suppress enemy ISR cedes the sensor-to-shooter race.

monthlyInsights.title: Drone ISR drives lethal counter-battery
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Persistent overhead ISR collapsed the time from firing to counter-fire — guns that lingered were destroyed.
experiment: Reduced firing-position occupation times and dispersed gun lines.
Automated move-after-fire prompts.
train: Shoot-and-scoot inside realistic counter-battery timelines.
Hide, survivability-move and hasty-occupation drills.
adjustSOP: Set maximum occupation times and mandatory displacement triggers in the gunnery SOP.

findings.text: Guns and armour avoided deploying under drone interdiction; those that lingered were found and struck — shoot-and-scoot is survival.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Persistent overhead ISR collapsed the time from firing to counter-fire — guns that lingered were destroyed.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Guns that linger are located and counter-fired within minutes, turning static gun lines into casualties rather than fire support.

monthlyInsights.title: Deep strike imposes dispersal on the enemy rear
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Holding rear logistics and airpower at persistent risk shaped adversary behaviour without ground action — but only with a fast deliberate-targeting cycle.
experiment: Standing deep-target list with refresh and BDA cadence.
Cross-cue between collection and deep fires.
train: Deliberate targeting boards working perishable deep targets.
BDA-driven re-attack decisions.
adjustSOP: Define deep-target nomination, approval and BDA cycle in the targeting SOP.

sources.label: Al Jazeera — Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries

findings.text: Deep strikes ranged ~1,300km to Saratov and Kirov, hitting refineries and pumping stations — a fast deep-target cycle imposes dispersal.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Holding rear logistics and airpower at persistent risk shaped adversary behaviour without ground action — but only with a fast deliberate-targeting cycle.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Without a fast deep-targeting cycle the enemy rear operates with impunity, sustaining the tempo of attacks on the formation.

monthlyInsights.title: Maintain warning under denied access
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Reduced inspection access widened the indications-and-warning gap — intelligence must keep functioning when its best sensors are denied.
experiment: Multi-source collection plans resilient to loss of any single feed.
Structured analytic techniques for high-ambiguity warning.
train: I&W and collection-management drills under denied-access conditions.
Indicator-based warning with explicit confidence.
adjustSOP: Build a denied-access collection-management annex into the intelligence SOP.

sources.label: ISW — how Iran hopes to control the Strait of Hormuz

findings.text: Iran normalised de facto chokepoint control through protraction and third-party workarounds — warning must track ambiguous, deniable coercion.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Reduced inspection access widened the indications-and-warning gap — intelligence must keep functioning when its best sensors are denied.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: When the best collection is denied, indications-and-warning gaps widen and the formation can be surprised by a developing threat.

monthlyInsights.title: Tactical EW counter-UAS — necessary, not sufficient
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Trench/vehicle jammers defeated most RF drones but were bypassed by fibre-optic FPV — EW must be paired with kinetic defeat and managed for fratricide.
experiment: Sub-unit EW kits paired with a kinetic last-ditch layer.
EW-fratricide management with friendly comms/UAS.
train: Layered counter-UAS drills with EW + kinetic defeat.
Spectrum management to avoid jamming own links.
adjustSOP: Define EW employment, de-confliction and the kinetic backstop in the counter-UAS SOP.

findings.text: The drone contest is persistent and mutual — EW alone is insufficient as the reconnaissance-strike advantage shifts continuously.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Trench/vehicle jammers defeated most RF drones but were bypassed by fibre-optic FPV — EW must be paired with kinetic defeat and managed for fratricide.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: EW alone gives a false sense of cover; once bypassed by wire-guided drones, unprotected nodes and troops are exposed.

monthlyInsights.title: Emitting command posts are quickly found and struck
monthlyInsights.soWhat: A CP was located and struck after its electromagnetic signature was detected — survivability now hinges on dispersion, displacement and low signature.
experiment: Dispersed/disaggregated CP layouts with remote antennas.
Low-signature and on-the-move C2 nodes.
train: CP displacement timings and silent-running drills.
Antenna remoting and emission control at the node.
adjustSOP: Mandate CP dispersion, displacement intervals and EMCON in the C2 SOP.

findings.text: Rear nodes and concentrations are exposed to persistent strike — dispersed, low-signature, displacing command posts survive.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: A CP was located and struck after its electromagnetic signature was detected — survivability now hinges on dispersion, displacement and low signature.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: An emitting, static command post is a located target — losing it decapitates the formation's decision-making at the worst moment.

monthlyInsights.title: Resilient PACE comms under jamming
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Mesh networking and alternate bearers sustained C2 through jamming/spoofing — a rehearsed PACE plan was decisive.
experiment: Mesh/alt-bearer and low-probability-of-intercept options.
Data-only fallbacks when voice is jammed.
train: PACE rehearsals under active jamming until bearer-switching is reflexive.
Degraded-comms mission command.
adjustSOP: Require a rehearsed PACE plan and bearer-switch criteria in unit signals SOPs.
monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Mesh networking and alternate bearers sustained C2 through jamming/spoofing — a rehearsed PACE plan was decisive.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Jamming and spoofing sever command links, so without rehearsed alternates the formation loses C2 in the decisive phase.

monthlyInsights.title: Counter-tunnel detection and breach is a deliberate task
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Robotics, sensing and engineered defeat — not improvisation — were needed to clear subterranean networks.
experiment: Robotics and sensing for tunnel detection and standoff defeat.
Counter-mobility integration with manoeuvre breaching.
train: Subterranean detection and breach as an engineer ICT serial.
Confined-space and degraded-comms operations.
adjustSOP: Add detection/breach task-org and safety to the engineer subterranean SOP.

findings.text: Tunnel-network destruction was a required, deliberate engineering task — resource detection and breach rather than improvising.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Robotics, sensing and engineered defeat — not improvisation — were needed to clear subterranean networks.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Subterranean defences neutralise surface overmatch; without deliberate detection and breach capability, clearance stalls and casualties mount.

monthlyInsights.title: Harden and disperse logistics and energy nodes
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Fixed logistics/energy nodes were lucrative, repeatable deep-strike targets — concentration is a vulnerability.
experiment: Dispersed, redundant node layouts with multiple supply routes.
Hardened/buried fuel and ammunition storage.
train: Node hardening, dispersal and route-redundancy drills.
Continuity-of-supply when a node is lost.
adjustSOP: Set dispersion/hardening standards for critical nodes in the sustainment SOP.

sources.label: Al Jazeera — Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries

findings.text: Systematic strikes hit refineries and pumping stations across five regions — fixed energy and logistics nodes are repeatable targets.
Mid-range drone strikes hit depots and supply routes lacking air-defence cover — disperse and cover critical nodes.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Fixed logistics/energy nodes were lucrative, repeatable deep-strike targets — concentration is a vulnerability.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Concentrated, fixed logistics and energy nodes are lucrative deep-strike targets whose loss halts sustainment for the whole formation.

monthlyInsights.title: Gun-based SHORAD revalidated against drones
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Self-propelled anti-aircraft guns achieved favourable cost-exchange against loitering munitions and drones — divested gun SHORAD has renewed value.
experiment: Gun SHORAD/C-UAS integrated into formation protection.
Cued gun engagement from passive/acoustic sensors.
train: SHORAD and counter-UAS gunnery against representative drone profiles.
Cost-per-kill-aware engagement decisions.
adjustSOP: Integrate gun air-defence into the protection plan and counter-UAS SOP.

findings.text: Drone interdiction constrained adversary artillery and armour movement — low-cost air defence over routes and positions is decisive.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Self-propelled anti-aircraft guns achieved favourable cost-exchange against loitering munitions and drones — divested gun SHORAD has renewed value.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Cheap drones impose an unfavourable cost-exchange on missile defences, leaving manoeuvre units exposed without affordable gun air defence.

monthlyInsights.title: Signature management as a survival skill
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Thermal and electromagnetic discipline materially reduced detection and engagement — detectability, not firepower, often decided survival.
experiment: Thermal/EM signature-reduction measures at section/platoon.
Self-audit tools to check own signature.
train: Signature-discipline drills (thermal, EM, visual, spoil).
Routine signature audits before occupation.
adjustSOP: Make signature management a graded standard with audits in unit SOPs.

findings.text: Eroding reconnaissance advantage shows detection drives loss — signature discipline materially reduces exposure and engagement.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Thermal and electromagnetic discipline materially reduced detection and engagement — detectability, not firepower, often decided survival.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Detectability drives loss as much as firepower; poor thermal and EM discipline gets a sub-unit found and struck.

monthlyInsights.title: Small uncrewed craft achieve sea denial
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Cheap explosive uncrewed surface vessels denied a conventional fleet freedom of action — small craft can impose outsized littoral effect.
experiment: Small-craft raiding and counter-USV concepts for chokepoints/littorals.
Distributed sensing for small fast surface threats.
train: Counter-USV defence of jetties, chokepoints and coastal nodes.
Small-craft raiding and withdrawal.
adjustSOP: Add counter-USV watch and engagement procedures to coastal/littoral SOPs.
monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Cheap explosive uncrewed surface vessels denied a conventional fleet freedom of action — small craft can impose outsized littoral effect.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Cheap uncrewed craft can deny a stronger force freedom of movement in confined waters, threatening coastal and chokepoint operations.

monthlyInsights.title: Raids overextend without a protected egress
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Egress under observation — not the assault — was the point of greatest risk for raiding forces.
experiment: Deception and multiple egress routes planned to the same weight as the assault.
Pre-positioned extraction and casualty plans.
train: Raid egress, break-clean and casualty extraction under observation.
Actions-on compromise during withdrawal.
adjustSOP: Require a rehearsed egress and extraction plan in the raid SOP, not just actions-on-objective.

sources.label: Al Jazeera — IDF pushes past the Litani

findings.text: Forces held a ~2,000 sq km belt and struck logistics/command at depth — egress back through contested depth is the point of risk.
Small-unit clashes over specific localities continued ~30km beyond the border — withdrawal through contested ground is exposed.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Egress under observation — not the assault — was the point of greatest risk for raiding forces.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: The withdrawal, not the assault, is the killing zone; an under-planned egress turns a successful raid into a loss.

monthlyInsights.title: Limited-objective action under ceasefire constraint
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Operations continued under a fragile ceasefire within tight political/ROE limits — tactical action had to be calibrated to escalation thresholds.
experiment: Escalation-control measures and clear decision rights at low echelon.
Scenario libraries for ROE-constrained actions.
train: ROE-constrained limited-objective operations with escalation control.
Junior-leader judgement against political thresholds.
adjustSOP: Embed ROE and escalation-control decision rights in the limited-operations SOP.

sources.label: Al Jazeera — IDF pushes past the Litani

findings.text: The truce was 'never observed in practice' and both sides cited breaches to justify action — operate with explicit escalation-control thresholds.
Continued operations under ceasefire set conditions for long-term control — calibrate tactical action to the political aim.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Operations continued under a fragile ceasefire within tight political/ROE limits — tactical action had to be calibrated to escalation thresholds.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Tactical action must stay inside tight political/ROE limits, so a junior leader's local decision can carry strategic consequences.

monthlyInsights.title: Rapid seize-and-hold of decisive terrain
monthlyInsights.soWhat: A fast crossing seized a dominating feature and consolidated before an effective counter could form — tempo plus rapid consolidation converted a raid into a holding gain.
experiment: Pre-packaged consolidation/counter-attack-defeat sets for seized objectives.
Rapid obstacle and counter-UAS emplacement on seizure.
train: Seize-and-hold with immediate consolidation against counter-attack.
Hand-over to follow-on forces.
adjustSOP: Add immediate-consolidation and counter-attack-defeat steps to the seize-and-hold SOP.

sources.label: Al Jazeera — IDF pushes past the Litani

findings.text: Beaufort Castle's seizure gave commanding observation over southern routes and supply lines — seize dominating terrain and consolidate to hold it.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: A fast crossing seized a dominating feature and consolidated before an effective counter could form — tempo plus rapid consolidation converted a raid into a holding gain.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Seizing key terrain is only half the task — without immediate consolidation the objective is lost to counter-attack.

monthlyInsights.title: Sustaining distributed action under blockade
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Operations persisted under a maritime near-blockade — distributed forces depended on resilient, low-signature sustainment.
experiment: Pre-positioned, cached and low-footprint resupply for distributed detachments.
Multiple/alternate lines of communication.
train: Sustainment under constrained LOCs and emission control.
Endurance planning when resupply is cut.
adjustSOP: Add low-signature, pre-positioned resupply to the distributed-operations SOP.

sources.label: NYT — Strait of Hormuz shipping insurance
ISW — how Iran hopes to control the Strait of Hormuz

findings.text: Chokepoint control persisted regardless of compliance and third-party workarounds emerged — plan distributed, alternate-route sustainment.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Operations persisted under a maritime near-blockade — distributed forces depended on resilient, low-signature sustainment.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: When lines of communication are constrained, a distributed force runs out of supply unless sustainment is pre-positioned and low-signature.

monthlyInsights.title: Close organic ISR and reachback gaps
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Small units operating independently lacked organic ISR and assured reachback, creating awareness gaps on non-permissive tasks.
experiment: Organic small-UAS and sensor packages held at detachment level.
Assured, low-signature reachback links.
train: Organic ISR employment and reachback drills on independent tasks.
Operating through contested links.
adjustSOP: Define organic-ISR holdings and reachback PACE for detached sub-units.

findings.text: Small-unit clashes over specific localities amid talks — detached forces need organic ISR to read a fragmented, contested area.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Small units operating independently lacked organic ISR and assured reachback, creating awareness gaps on non-permissive tasks.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Detached sub-units without organic ISR and assured reachback operate partially blind in non-permissive areas.

monthlyInsights.title: Mission command rewards disciplined initiative
monthlyInsights.soWhat: Decentralised initiative within intent outpaced the adversary's decision cycle where centralised control would have been too slow.
experiment: Explicit decision rights and commander's-intent formats for junior leaders.
Rehearsed degraded-comms initiative.
train: Mission-command serials that force decisions without higher direction.
Acting on intent under uncertainty.
adjustSOP: State decision rights and intent-driven authorities in unit command SOPs.

findings.text: Geolocated counter-responses intercepted predictable infiltration — disciplined initiative and tempo beat scripted, centralised action.

monthlyInsights.observedInTheatre: Decentralised initiative within intent outpaced the adversary's decision cycle where centralised control would have been too slow.
monthlyInsights.tacticalProblem: Centralised control is too slow for fleeting opportunities; without trained, intent-driven initiative the unit forfeits tempo.

weeklyCapabilitySignals._doc: Per-week capability observations that drive computed heat & trend. id=capability, t=theatre, i=intensity(1-3), x=type(emp employment | ctr counter | prolif proliferation | super supersession).

weeklyReports.bluf: RUS-UKR remains the dominant high-intensity theatre with intensifying deep strikes; Levant theatres show diverging trajectories as the Lebanon ceasefire frays while Gaza edges toward contested de-escalation. Iran standoff stays volatile; Thai-Cambodian border tension is localised but unresolved.

RU_UA.progressToDate: Front broadly static; Russia presses Donetsk axis with marginal gains at high cost.
RU_UA.bluf: Russia opens the reporting period with a surge of long-range strikes on Ukrainian energy and logistics while grinding forward around Pokrovsk.
keyDevelopments: Mass drone-and-missile salvo targets western Ukraine power infrastructure.
Incremental Russian advance west of Avdiivka; Ukrainian lines hold under pressure.
Ukraine strikes a Black Sea fuel depot with long-range drones.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: Highest tempo of the period: combined cruise/ballistic/drone salvos stress Ukrainian air defence interceptor stocks.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Improved Ukrainian deep-strike targeting of rear fuel nodes; Russian ISR-strike loop tightening near the front.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Marginal Russian gains; no operational breakthrough. Ukrainian reserves committed locally.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Interceptor expenditure outpacing resupply; civil energy hardening lagging demand.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: Russian artillery ammunition advantage persists; Ukrainian shell flow steadier than 2024.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Ukraine reorganising drone units under unified command; Russian theatre C2 stable.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Energy-grid strike campaign resumes at scale
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: The strike surge is the week's decisive effect, driving air-defence depletion and civil-protection strain.

RU_UA.watchAreas: Watch the 14 Apr air-defence resupply decision in Washington and whether Kyiv commits new long-range systems to counter-strikes.

IL_LB.progressToDate: Ceasefire nominally holding since late 2024 but violation rate rising.
IL_LB.bluf: Cross-border exchanges tick up as Hezbollah reconstitution and Israeli interdiction strikes erode the ceasefire's credibility.
keyDevelopments: Israeli strike on a claimed weapons-transfer site in the Bekaa.
Limited Hezbollah probing fire near the Blue Line.
Lebanese army deployment south of the Litani reported incomplete.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: Targeted Israeli strikes continue against reconstitution; Hezbollah responses limited and signalling-oriented.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Israeli ISR dominance over southern Lebanon sustained; pattern-of-life targeting evident.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Static front; Lebanese army deployment gaps create ambiguity zones.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Northern Israeli communities partially repopulated; shelter posture eased but reversible.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: Hezbollah resupply via Syria constrained but not severed.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Ceasefire monitoring mechanism functioning but slow to adjudicate violations.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: ISR-cued interdiction defines the ceasefire dynamic
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: Israeli intelligence dominance is enabling the strikes that most strain the truce this week.

IL_LB.watchAreas: Watch the ceasefire monitoring committee session expected mid-month and any Lebanese army redeployment announcement.

IL_GZ.progressToDate: Major combat reduced; localised clearance operations and aid disputes continue.
IL_GZ.bluf: Operations shift to targeted raids and stabilisation friction as negotiations over a phased arrangement stall and resume.
keyDevelopments: Targeted raid against a re-forming militant cell in central Gaza.
Aid throughput rises then falls amid crossing disputes.
Indirect talks on a phased framework resume via mediators.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: Lower volume, precision-focused; episodic flare-ups around clearance.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Tunnel-mapping and HUMINT-led targeting continue to drive raids.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Israeli forces hold corridors; militant freedom of movement constrained.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Civilian-harm mitigation under scrutiny; force protection against IEDs a focus.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: Humanitarian logistics remain the central friction point.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Phased-framework talks resume
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: The negotiation track is the week's most consequential lever over the trajectory.

IL_GZ.watchAreas: Watch the mediator-hosted session and any announcement on hostage-for-pause sequencing.

IL_US_IR.progressToDate: No direct state-on-state war; proxy friction and nuclear diplomacy oscillate.
IL_US_IR.bluf: Indirect signalling dominates as enrichment concerns and maritime harassment keep the standoff hot but below open conflict.
keyDevelopments: IAEA flags reduced monitoring access at a key site.
Tanker harassment incident reported in the Strait of Hormuz.
US carrier presence sustained in the Gulf.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: No direct exchange; proxy rocket activity low this week.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Enrichment ambiguity central; reduced inspector access raises I&W risk.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Naval posturing in the Gulf; no ground dimension.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: US carrier sustainment and basing access stable.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Diplomatic back-channels reportedly active; deterrence signalling deliberate.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Monitoring-access reduction raises I&W risk
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: Loss of inspection clarity is the week's most strategically significant shift.

IL_US_IR.watchAreas: Watch the IAEA Board statement and any scheduling of indirect US-Iran talks.

TH_KH.progressToDate: Border tension localised around disputed temple areas; no escalation to sustained combat.
TH_KH.bluf: Border friction persists at low level with troop build-ups near contested zones but functioning bilateral channels.
keyDevelopments: Limited troop reinforcement reported near a disputed border sector.
Bilateral border committee meeting scheduled.
Nationalist rhetoric elevated on both sides domestically.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: No significant exchange; small-arms incidents only.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Mutual surveillance of border build-ups; low transparency.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Localised force concentration near contested terrain.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Civil-defence measures minimal; population movement limited.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Bilateral mechanisms functioning; domestic politics a complicating input.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Border committee meeting scheduled
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: The diplomatic channel is the most decision-relevant development this week.

TH_KH.watchAreas: Watch the bilateral border committee outcome and any change in troop posture at the disputed sector.

weeklyReports.bluf: Strike intensity in RUS-UKR remains elevated but plateaus; Lebanon deteriorates further on a sharper Israeli strike; Gaza talks gain modest traction. Iran standoff steady-volatile; Thai-Cambodian channel produces a tentative understanding.

RU_UA.progressToDate: Front static; strike tempo high but no new operational gains.
RU_UA.bluf: Strike campaign continues at high tempo while ground lines hold; Ukrainian deep strikes increasingly target Russian logistics.
keyDevelopments: Ukrainian strike damages a rail logistics hub in occupied territory.
Russian glide-bomb employment intensifies along the Kharkiv axis.
Western pledge of additional interceptors announced.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: Glide bombs remain Russia's high-leverage tool; Ukrainian deep strikes maturing.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Ukrainian targeting of rail nodes shows improved deep-ISR fusion.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: No operational movement; attritional pressure continues.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Interceptor pledge eases near-term air-defence risk.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: Russian rail dependency emerges as a targetable vulnerability.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Both sides' theatre C2 stable; tempo unchanged.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Russian rail logistics struck as a system vulnerability
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: Targeting sustainment, not the front line, is the week's most strategically meaningful shift.

RU_UA.watchAreas: Watch interceptor delivery timelines and whether Ukraine sustains the rail-interdiction campaign.

IL_LB.progressToDate: Violation tempo rising; ceasefire architecture under visible strain.
IL_LB.bluf: A higher-profile Israeli strike and a Hezbollah response push the ceasefire to its most strained point of the month.
keyDevelopments: Israeli strike kills a mid-level Hezbollah commander.
Hezbollah fires in retaliation; limited and signalled.
Monitoring committee convenes amid mutual complaints.

domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Decapitation strike reflects continued Israeli targeting penetration.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Northern Israeli alert posture raised; shelters reactivated locally.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Decapitation strike triggers measured Hezbollah response
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: The strike-response cycle is the week's defining escalation risk.

IL_LB.watchAreas: Watch the monitoring committee's adjudication and any threshold rhetoric on resumed operations.

IL_GZ.progressToDate: Negotiation track advances; localised operations continue.
IL_GZ.bluf: Mediators report progress on a phased framework, nudging the theatre toward contested de-escalation despite ongoing friction.
keyDevelopments: Mediators announce convergence on a phased sequence.
Aid throughput stabilises at higher level for several days.
Sporadic clearance operations continue in the south.

domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Verification mechanisms become a negotiation focus.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Corridor control maintained; reduced clearance tempo.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Civilian-harm scrutiny continues to shape operations.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Negotiation channel gains structure and momentum.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Convergence on a phased framework
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: Diplomatic convergence is the most consequential driver of de-escalation this week.

IL_GZ.watchAreas: Watch the announced framework's first implementation milestone and the linked hostage-release sequencing.

IL_US_IR.progressToDate: Standoff steady; diplomacy and deterrence in tension.
IL_US_IR.bluf: A tentative agreement to schedule indirect talks steadies the standoff even as enrichment concerns persist.
keyDevelopments: Indirect talks tentatively scheduled via a third party.
No new maritime incidents reported this week.
US sustains Gulf force posture.

domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Enrichment ambiguity persists pending talks.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: US basing and carrier sustainment unchanged.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Diplomatic track gains a tentative schedule.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Indirect talks tentatively scheduled
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: Scheduling diplomacy is the week's key inflection in an otherwise static standoff.

IL_US_IR.watchAreas: Watch confirmation of the talks date and any pre-talks enrichment signalling.

TH_KH.progressToDate: Bilateral channel yields a tentative understanding on patrols.
TH_KH.bluf: The border committee produces a tentative understanding to limit patrols near the disputed sector, easing tension modestly.
keyDevelopments: Border committee announces a patrol-limitation understanding.
Partial troop drawdown reported at one sector.
Nationalist rhetoric softens slightly.

domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Mutual monitoring continues; transparency improving marginally.
domainAnalysis.Command & Control: Bilateral mechanism delivers a concrete output.

selectedDevelopmentPill.headline: Patrol-limitation understanding reached
selectedDevelopmentPill.rationale: A concrete bilateral output is the week's most de-escalatory development.

TH_KH.watchAreas: Watch implementation of the patrol understanding and any ASEAN facilitation offer.

weeklyReports.bluf: RUS-UKR sees a localised Russian push with modest gains; Lebanon stabilises slightly after committee intervention; Gaza framework hits an implementation snag. Iran talks confirmed; Thai-Cambodian understanding holds.

RU_UA.progressToDate: Russia achieves a tactical gain near a key town; lines otherwise hold.
RU_UA.bluf: Russia concentrates forces for a localised assault, taking a contested town while strike tempo stays high.
keyDevelopments: Russian forces seize a contested town on the Donetsk axis.
Ukraine conducts a deep strike on an airbase in occupied Crimea.
Heavy drone duel along the Dnipro line.

domainAnalysis.Fires & Strikes: Sustained two-way strike tempo; Crimea airbase struck.
domainAnalysis.Intelligence: Ukrainian deep-strike ISR continues to find high-value rear targets.
domainAnalysis.Manoeuvre: Localised Russian gain through force concentration; costly.
domainAnalysis.Protection: Air-defence holding with new interceptors; rear bases vulnerable.
domainAnalysis.Sustainment: Russian massing reflects sustainment prioritisation on one axis.
